title: HBO documentary series is scouting Cambridge for ‘unique’ neighbor stories
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: HBO's series 'Neighbors' is casting in Cambridge for unique neighbor stories ahead of its second season.
The show is searching for stories involving disputes or unusual relationships, such as noise and property issues.
Cambridge residents can submit their neighbor stories through the show's casting website for potential inclusion.

title: The CapeFLYER train between Boston and Cape Cod returns for the summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The CapeFLYER train resumes its 13th season, offering weekend service from South Station to Hyannis starting May 22.
Over 10,000 CapeFLYER tickets were sold in 2025, with a 9% ridership increase reported by CCRTA administrator John Kennedy.
Round-trip tickets cost $40, with free rides for children under 11; bikes and pets are welcome without extra charge.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about the forecast for Memorial Day weekend
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston's Memorial Day weekend forecast expects cooler air with highs in the 60s and mostly cloudy skies.
Rain is anticipated to arrive late Saturday, with the highest chance of precipitation on Sunday.
Weather conditions are predicted to improve by Memorial Day, becoming drier and less rainy.

title: Jarren Duran says he’s ‘willing to get hurt to make a play for my pitchers’ after Red Sox win
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jarren Duran hit a triple and home run in Red Sox's 4-3 win over the Royals.
Duran emphasized his commitment to making defensive plays for pitchers, regardless of potential injury.
Boston Red Sox completed a sweep of the Royals and have a 22-27 season record.

title: A two-bedroom condo steps from Roslindale Square — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Two-bedroom condo near Roslindale Square built in 2018 with 727 square feet.
Monthly fee of $358 covers water, sewer, insurance, and maintenance for the condo.
The condo's annual taxes are $6,288, excluding the residential exemption.

title: Local obituary: Peter Jennings, 35, Wellesley father and standout athlete at Wellesley High and Middlebury
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Peter Aliber Jennings, 35, a former Wellesley High standout athlete, passed away after battling glioblastoma.
Jennings is survived by his wife Julia, children Henry and Phoebe, and an extended family.
Visitation and funeral services are scheduled at St. Andrew’s Church in Wellesley on May 26-27.

title: What to know about the USS Constitution ahead of Boston’s parade of tall ships
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The USS Constitution will lead Sail Boston 250's parade from July 11-16, celebrating 250 years of U.S. maritime history.
Built in 1797, the USS Constitution is the world's oldest commissioned warship, known for victories that strengthened U.S. identity.
Currently undergoing maintenance, the USS Constitution will be restored to ensure its participation in the bicentennial event.

title: T police release video of ‘anti-social, violent’ suspect on the Orange Line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA Transit Police are seeking public assistance to identify a man who kicked a train window on the Orange Line.
The suspect reportedly exhibited violent behavior and assaulted a woman on an Orange Line train on May 8.
Authorities released video footage from Jackson Square and urge anyone with information to contact 617-222-1050.

title: Man arrested after allegedly grabbing onto child, becoming ‘aggressive’ on Green Line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 33-year-old man was arrested for aggressive behavior towards a woman and her child on the Green Line.
The incident occurred after the child's mother declined to give the man her phone number, police report says.
The suspect, who appeared intoxicated, was apprehended near Chauncy/Summer Street; charges are pending arraignment.

title: 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seven MassDOT employees are on leave after being accused of falsifying timesheets for overtime pay.
A Boston 25 News investigation revealed employees leaving before shifts ended and earning excessive overtime.
MassDOT has launched its own investigation into the overtime misuse at the District Six facility in Charlestown.
